channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Delhi prohibits LPG cylinder sales from storage points, conducts multiple inspections
item.description: The Delhi government has announced a ban on the sale of LPG cylinders directly from godowns within the city limits, citing safety and regulatory concerns. Authorities conducted 17 raids across various...

item.title: Nineteen-year-old charged after violent incidents before Irish Cup semi-final in Belfast
item.description: Police in Belfast have charged a 19-year-old man following violent scenes ahead of the Irish Cup semi-final between Cliftonville and Dungannon Swifts at Windsor Park. The events unfolded during a publ...

item.title: NASA Shares First Images from Artemis II and Earth from Lunar Orbit
item.description: NASA has released the first images from the Artemis II mission, which currently has four astronauts orbiting the Moon. The images show Earth in the background, appearing small and distant, emphasizing...

item.title: Eastern Cape records over ten fatalities on roads during Easter weekend
item.description: Since the start of the Easter period, more than ten people have lost their lives on Eastern Cape roads, the provincial transport department confirmed on Sunday. The deadliest accident occurred in the ...

item.title: Senator Ayo Adeseun Steps Down From Mayegun Title Over Palace Disputes
item.description: Senator Ayo Adeseun, wey be former lawmaker and senior APC member, don resign from him post as Mayegun of Ogbomoso land. Dis matter follow after Soun of Ogbomoso, Oba Afolabi Olaoye Ghandi, suspend am...

item.title: Tokyo’s Radio Device Recreates Historic Broadcasts for Memory Therapy
item.description: A Tokyo-based agency, TBWA\HAKUHODO, has developed an innovative device called the 'Radio Time Machine' that recreates radio broadcasts from past years. The device resembles a classic mid-century radi...

item.title: Pietermaritzburg High Court sets aside Solbeth's subpoena against Auditor-General as abuse of process
item.description: The Pietermaritzburg High Court has ruled in favour of the Auditor-General of South Africa (AGSA), setting aside a subpoena issued by security firm Solbeth Security Protection Services. The court desc...

item.title: Rivers Traditional Rulers Launch Peace and Development Group, Deny Political Motives
item.description: Concerned Rivers Chiefs For Peace And Development has explained that the formation of the group was not politically motivated and it does not have any links to political parties or candidates in River...

item.title: Weekly Highlights from Moss & Fog: Design, Nature, and Visionary Ideas
item.description: This week’s edition of Fog Signals from Moss & Fog (March 30 – April 5, 2026) gathers fascinating stories from around the world, blending art, design, science, and imagination. The issue opens with a ...

item.title: Senate stops Technical University of Kenya plan to defer retirees’ pension payments
item.description: Kenyan senators have turned down a contentious proposal that sought to delay pension payments for retired staff of the Technical University of Kenya (TUK) until the year 2030. The proposal, pushed for...

item.title: Concerns Rise Over Detention of Migrant Children with Disputed Ages Under UK Removal Programme
item.description: More than seventy unaccompanied minors from conflict zones such as Eritrea, Sudan and Afghanistan have been detained in adult centres by the UK Home Office under its so-called 'one in, one out' scheme...

item.title: Ukraine faces severe manpower challenges amid ongoing war with Russia
item.description: Ukraine is struggling with a major conscription crisis as the war against Russia enters its fourth year. Defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov revealed that around two million Ukrainians have evaded mobil...

item.title: Taber, Alberta, Wins 2026 Kraft Hockeyville Title Over Tumbler Ridge, B.C.
item.description: Taber, Alberta, has been named the 2026 Kraft Hockeyville champion, beating out Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, for the coveted community hockey title. The announcement came on April 4, 2026, as part...

item.title: A warm and witty space adventure about connection and survival
item.description: The article by Kibo Ngowi from the Mail & Guardian explores the film Project Hail Mary, directed by Phil Lord and Christopher Miller and starring Ryan Gosling and Sandra Hüller. Initially, it appears ...

item.title: Modern three-bedroom townhouse in Campbell blends elegance, technology and comfort
item.description: A newly featured townhouse in Campbell’s Bijou development highlights the blend of modern design and comfortable living in one of Canberra’s most desirable suburbs. The three-bedroom, two-bathroom res...

item.title: Kajiado Businesswoman Wins KSh 9.5 Million After Months of Family Betting Practice
item.description: A 50-year-old woman from Kajiado County, Caroline Mmbone, has become a millionaire after winning KSh 9.5 million in the SportPesa Midweek Jackpot. Caroline, a mother of six and small-scale businesswom...

item.title: Traffic and Safety Advisory for N3 Toll Route During Easter Weekend Return
item.description: As the Easter weekend winds down, motorists travelling on the N3 Toll Route between Cedara in KwaZulu-Natal and Heidelberg in Gauteng are advised to prepare for heavy traffic and variable weather cond...

item.title: Syrian Government Accounts Breach Reveals Critical Cybersecurity Weaknesses
item.description: In March 2026, several verified Syrian government accounts on X, including those linked to the presidency, Central Bank, and ministries, were hijacked. The compromised accounts posted messages support...

item.title: LTFRB summons bus driver for running red light in viral video
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) has summoned both the operator and the driver of a passenger bus that was seen running a red traffic light in a viral video. The board ...

item.title: Report shows high number of Kerala election candidates facing criminal cases and rising wealth levels
item.description: The Association for Democratic Reforms (ADR) has released a detailed report ahead of the 2026 Kerala Assembly elections, revealing that a significant portion of candidates have criminal backgrounds an...
